seoTitle: How to Combine Multiple Excel Files in Power BI Easily
metaDescription: Learn how to combine multiple Excel files into one Power BI query, saving time when consolidating budgets, reports, or data from several sources.
intro: Manually pasting together data from a dozen separate Excel files is tedious and error-prone, especially when new files keep arriving. This guide shows how to combine multiple Excel files in Power BI using a single query, so all your source files feed into one clean table. It's particularly useful for finance and operations teams consolidating reports from different departments.

steps.heading: Prepare your Excel files
steps.body: Store the files you want to combine in a single folder, and make sure they share a consistent structure so the combination step works smoothly.

steps.heading: Access the Power BI query editor
steps.body: Use Get Data and choose the Folder option to point Power BI at the folder containing all your Excel files.

steps.heading: Combine the files
steps.body: Use the Combine & Transform option in the query editor to merge the contents of every file in the folder into a single unified table.

steps.heading: Finalize data transformations
steps.body: Clean up column names, remove duplicate headers, and adjust data types so the combined table is consistent and ready to use.

steps.heading: Refresh as new files are added
steps.body: Because the query points to the folder rather than individual files, refreshing the report will automatically pick up any new files added later.

faq.question: Do all the Excel files need identical column structures?
faq.answer: It works best when files share the same columns and layout, since mismatched structures can cause errors or missing data during the combine step.

faq.question: What happens if I add a new Excel file to the folder later?
faq.answer: As long as the query is connected to the folder, refreshing the report will automatically include the new file's data in the combined table.

faq.question: Can I combine files from different folders?
faq.answer: Power BI's folder-based combine feature works on a single folder, though you can use additional query steps to bring in files from more than one location.

faq.question: Will combining large numbers of files slow down my report?
faq.answer: Combining many large files can increase refresh times, so it's worth monitoring performance and considering filtering unnecessary data early in the process.
